one_time_description: Loss on write-down of tangible capital assets - two housing units under construction that were never delivered by the contractor
economic_notes: Operates Buffalo Crossing Convenience & Gas Bar. Holds 8.3% interest in Keseechiwan Holdings Inc. (Swift Current Casino Development) and 4.5% interest in IMI Brokerage Limited Partnership. CMHC Phase 37 approved post year-end for 3 housing units at $542,000. Received additional prevention funding of $696,000 post year-end from Canadian Human Rights Tribunal order with ISC.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 (not 2024). Consolidated entity includes Standing Buffalo Dakota First Nation, CMHC Housing, Standing Buffalo Care Home Inc., Dakota Development Corporation, and Buffalo Crossing Convenience & Gas Bar. Net debt position of $2,647,462. Loss on write-down of $320,164 for two undelivered housing units. ISC funding per confirmation was $14,182,298 but recognized revenue is $11,157,566 after deferred revenue adjustments. CMHC rent and subsidy included in total government transfers. Remuneration schedule not included in provided text. OSR calculated as total revenue minus ISC ($11,157,566) minus CMHC subsidy/rent/RRAP ($663,521+$303,305) minus FHQ Tribal Council ($502,774) minus Sports Canada ($89,663) minus Justice Canada ($1,819) = approximately $5,515,377; however exact government transfer breakdown requires segment detail not fully provided.
